title: Surf Lessons at Mount Maunganui
meta_desc: Learn to surf with your family at Mount Maunganui, one of NZ's best learner beaches. Prices from $80/child, $95/adult for 2-hour lessons. Suitable for kids 7+.
intro: Imagine catching your first wave together as a family at one of New Zealand's most iconic beaches. Mount Maunganui in Tauranga offers an ideal spot for beginners to learn surfing. With its consistent small to medium waves, sandy bottom, and lifeguard presence, it's perfect for kids and adults alike to take their first surfing lessons.

sections.heading: What to Expect
sections.body:
Taking a surf lesson at Mount Maunganui is an exhilarating experience for the whole family. The 2-hour group lessons are comprehensive, covering the basics of surfing, including paddling, popping up, and balancing. The instructors are experienced and patient, ensuring that everyone feels comfortable and supported throughout the lesson.

The beach itself is very family-friendly, with plenty of space to relax and watch your loved ones learn to surf. The surf schools provide all the necessary equipment, including soft-top surfboards and wetsuits, which are perfect for beginners.

sections.heading: Age Suitability and Value for Money
sections.body:
The minimum age for surf lessons at Mount Maunganui is 7 years old, making it an excellent activity for families with older children. The price is $80 per child and $95 per adult, which is reasonable considering the quality of the lessons and the equipment provided.

In terms of value for money, the lessons are definitely worth the cost. Not only will you and your family learn a new skill, but you'll also have a fun and memorable experience together.

sections.heading: Insider Tips and Best Time to Visit
sections.body:
To make the most of your surf lesson experience, it's best to visit during the summer months (December to February) when the waves are generally smaller and more suitable for beginners. It's also a good idea to book your lesson early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the strong midday sun.

Additionally, be sure to arrive at least 30 minutes before your lesson to fill out any necessary paperwork and get changed into your wetsuit.
list: Book early morning or late afternoon lessons

sections.heading: Booking and Saving Money
sections.body:
To book your surf lesson, simply visit the website of your chosen surf school and follow the online booking process. Some surf schools may offer discounts for larger groups or families, so it's worth inquiring about any available deals.

You can also save money by booking a private lesson for your family, which can sometimes work out cheaper than individual group lessons.
list: Book online through the surf school's website

sections.heading: Downsides for Families
sections.body:
One of the downsides of taking a surf lesson at Mount Maunganui is that the beach can get quite crowded during peak season. This may mean that you have to park a short distance from the beach and walk with your surf gear.

Additionally, the lessons may not be suitable for very young children (under 7 years old), so you may need to make alternative arrangements for them while you and your older kids take the lesson.
